depression.description: I often feel like I'm just not good enough, especially when I compare myself to my colleagues. It's like no matter what I do, I can't shake this feeling of being a failure.
I constantly worry that if I don't meet everyone's expectations, I'll let them down. This fear makes it hard for me to take risks or ask for help.
Whenever I receive feedback, I can't help but dwell on the negative parts. It feels like I focus too much on what I did wrong, which just deepens my sadness and self-doubt.
I find it really difficult to open up about my feelings. I worry that if I show any weakness, people will think less of me or judge me.

Delusion.description: I've been having these recurring nightmares where I feel completely unprepared for important evaluations. They leave me feeling anxious and worried about how others perceive me.
I often catch myself ruminating about what my coworkers might be saying behind my back. It makes me feel anxious and fearful, impacting my confidence at work.
I have this deep-seated belief that I can't rely on anyone but myself. It feels like I have to handle everything alone, which just adds to my stress and sense of isolation.
